context: Checkpoint 2026-04-30T19:10Z after continuing Homestead/Studio plugin-surface slices. Branch develop is synchronized with origin/develop at ade09b71 unless a later handoff checkpoint commit is created. Disk remains constrained but stable at about 9.2GB free on /workspaces/refarm; no cleanup was run because free space is stable and caches are useful. Generated sqlite worker diff appeared after apps/dev build and was restored because it was the known build artifact flip, not intentional work.
current_tasks: Pushed CI-green commit b644b53c `feat(homestead): render plugin surfaces`: Homestead now calls optional plugin hook `renderHomesteadSurface` for trusted manifest-declared surfaces. Added `surface-renderer` normalization: plain string and `{ text }` become text writes; `{ html }` is explicit trusted HTML. Exported via `@refarm.dev/homestead/sdk` and documented in `packages/homestead/README.md`. CI run 25182811076 succeeded.

Pushed CI-green commit 6a56d601 `feat(examples): add executable multi-surface plugin`: `examples/multi-surface-plugin` now has executable `src/index.mjs` implementing `renderHomesteadSurface` and `summarizeTerminalStream`; manifest entry points to source so no generated `dist/` edits are needed. `packages/plugin-manifest` tests prove the manifest remains valid and executable. CI run 25183192735 succeeded.

Pushed CI-green commit ade09b71 `feat(app-dev): render executable stream surface`: the internal Studio stream demo plugin now implements `renderHomesteadSurface`, returning a real plugin-provided stream cockpit panel in the `streams` slot. Added `@refarm.dev/homestead/sdk/surface-renderer` subpath export/path mappings so apps/dev can import render request/result types without importing the full SDK/Shell (which previously triggered locale import resolution errors). CI run 25183938442 succeeded.

Important resolved gotcha: importing types from `@refarm.dev/homestead/sdk` in apps/dev pulled `Shell.ts` through TS path mapping and reintroduced `Cannot find module '@refarm.dev/locales/en.json'`. Use `@refarm.dev/homestead/sdk/surface-renderer` for renderer-only types, analogous to `surface-inspector` consumers.

Latest local validations for ade09b71 passed before push: `npm --prefix packages/homestead run build`, `npm --prefix apps/dev test -- stream-demo.test.ts`, `npm --prefix apps/dev run type-check`, `npm --prefix apps/dev run build`, `npm run project:validate --silent`, and `git diff --check`. Push hook had non-blocking local lint timeout/unit warning, but CI completed successfully.
blockers: Disk remains 99% used on the host mount with about 9.2GB free. Stable; no cleanup needed now. Check before broad builds. Use `npm run clean:light --silent` only if generated artifacts grow meaningfully or free space drops near 2-3GB.

Builds can rewrite `validations/sqlite-benchmark/browser/public/sqlite3-worker1.mjs` by inlining the LANG expression; this happened after apps/dev build and was restored. Continue to inspect and `git restore` unless intentionally working on that validation artifact.
Rust target dirs were previously cleaned. Avoid broad Rust commands unless Rust is touched; use scoped commands from AGENTS.md.
Known Vite browser externalization warnings for `node:fs/promises` and `node:path` remain non-blocking for apps/dev/apps/me builds.
Known moderate npm audit findings remain non-blocking: Astro, DOMPurify, PostCSS, uuid transitive chains; 0 high/critical in pre-push/CI checks.
next_actions: First command after compaction: `git status --branch --short && df -h /workspaces/refarm && git log --oneline -12`.
Commit/push this handoff checkpoint if desired after writing it, then watch CI if a push is made.

Next best atomic slice: add a small Homestead/AppDev integration test that proves `StudioShell` preserves both the plugin-provided executable stream surface and Homestead's built-in `[data-refarm-stream-panel]` live stream observer in the same `streams` slot.

Next plugin daily-driver slice: evolve `renderStudioStreamSurfaceDemo(...)` from static panel to a host-owned presenter contract (e.g. DOM event/action placeholders or surface metadata) without putting app-specific behavior in Homestead.
Next docs slice: update `docs/EXTENSIBILITY_MODEL.md` to describe the new `renderHomesteadSurface` hook and the new renderer-only subpath import boundary.
Next diagnostics slice: show which mounted surfaces are executable-rendered versus DOM wrapper-only in `/surfaces`, but keep presenter logic in apps/dev and Homestead helpers semantic/read-only.

If future apps/social starts, consume `bootStudioRuntime(...)`, `setupStudioShell(...)`, `createStudioPluginHandle(...)`/`createHomesteadSurfacePluginHandle(...)`, and renderer-only subpaths rather than importing the full Homestead SDK in app internals.
open_questions: Should `renderHomesteadSurface` become a formal manifest-declared API/capability beyond the current `ui:panel:render` capability, or is the method convention enough until more real plugins exercise it?

Should plugin-provided `{ html }` be sanitized or restricted further before broader external plugin enablement? Current gate relies on Homestead trust activation; plain string/`{ text }` remains safe text path.
Should `@refarm.dev/homestead/sdk/surface-renderer` be documented alongside `surface-inspector` as a preferred narrow import for app internals?

Should the Studio stream surface eventually consume live stream state directly via a host callback/context, or should Homestead remain the sole stream observer and executable surfaces only render static plugin affordances initially?
Do apps/dev and apps/me need shared TS path generation for Homestead subpaths to avoid manual path-map drift?
key_decisions_pending: Whether to formalize `renderHomesteadSurface` in plugin manifest docs now or wait for one more executable plugin surface.
Whether the next lane should be integration proof in Homestead tests, docs model update, or `/surfaces` executable-rendered diagnostics.
Whether to introduce a centralized app tsconfig path preset for Homestead subpaths.
Whether to schedule cleanup soon; current disk is stable, so no cleanup was run.
